events.title: World News in Brief: Sudan and Haiti updates, Afghan women’s rights
events.description: The United Nations is raising alarm over continued drone attacks in Sudan’s Darfur region after multiple strikes reportedly killed civilians and intensified fears for communities already trapped by the brutal conflict between rival militaries.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Sudan and Haiti updates, Afghan women’s rights

events.title: WHO chief calls for urgent Ebola action and pandemic preparedness
events.description: The recent Ebola and hantavirus outbreaks demonstrate that the world is still vulnerable to rapidly spreading infectious diseases, Tedros Ghebreyesus, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), warned on Saturday at the close of the 79th World Health Assembly in Geneva.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO chief calls for urgent Ebola action and pandemic preparedness

events.title: 5 people trapped in Laos cave found alive, 2 still missing
events.description: Five of seven people trapped in a flooded cave for a week in Laos were found alive on Wednesday, Laotian and Thai rescuers said. Seven Laotian villagers entered the cave in central Xaysomboun province on May 20. They were searching for gold but instead got trapped after heavy rain triggered flash flooding, blocking their exit.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 5 people trapped in Laos cave found alive, 2 still missing

events.title: International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak
events.description: As a deadly Ebola strain continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), with cases confirmed in neighbouring Uganda, the UN aviation agency is urging governments and flight operators to closely follow guidelines put in place following the COVID-19 pandemic.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak

events.title: WHO chief calls for DRC ceasefire to tackle Ebola outbreak
events.description: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warns of ‘catastrophic collision of disease and conflict’, as Uganda closes border with DRC The head of the World Health Organization has called for an immediate ceasefire in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo to help tackle the Ebola outbreak there, as Uganda closed its border with its neighbour in an effort to stop the spread. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus posted on social media that the region was in the midst of a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict with the Ebola outbreak in Ituri province outpacing the response”. Tedros said on Monday that he would travel to the DRC this week. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO chief calls for DRC ceasefire to tackle Ebola outbreak

events.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns
events.description: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday warned that eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict” as a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak outpaces containment efforts in a region already battered by armed violence, mass displacement and acute hunger.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns

events.title: Right to strike protected under key labour treaty, says UN World Court
events.description: The UN International Court of Justice (ICJ) on Thursday ruled that the right to strike is protected under a core International Labour Organization (ILO) convention, in a landmark advisory opinion settling a long-running dispute between workers and employers worldwide.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Right to strike protected under key labour treaty, says UN World Court

events.title: Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave
events.description: The search is continuing for a further two villagers who are still missing, rescuers say.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave

events.title: Hunger increasingly used as weapon of war as ‘food-related violence’ surges, analysis shows
events.description: More than 20,000 attacks on markets, farmland and food distribution systems have been recorded since 2018 Hunger is being increasingly exploited as a weapon of war with more than 20,000 documented incidents of “food-related violence” in the past eight years, new analysis reveals. Attacks include 1,261 strikes on markets used by families for daily groceries and 863 incidents in which food distribution systems were targeted and workers killed. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hunger increasingly used as weapon of war as ‘food-related violence’ surges, analysis shows

events.title: Five villagers found alive in Laos cave as search continues for two missing
events.description: Video appears to show divers discovering group sitting on a rock surrounded by flood water Rescuers have reached five of seven people who have been trapped for a week in a flooded, remote cave in Laos, after days spent navigating narrow, inundated passageways amid persistent rain. Video footage shared on social media by rescue divers showed the five men crouched together on a rocky ledge in a dark cave chamber, surrounded by muddy water. “There’s no need to cry,” one of the rescue team told the men, who first entered the cave on Wednesday. Two men remain unaccounted for. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Five villagers found alive in Laos cave as search continues for two missing

events.title: Wars and geopolitical divisions constitute ‘dangerous erosion’ of  world order, warns UN chief
events.description: The UN Charter is facing one of its gravest tests in decades, Secretary-General António Guterres told the Security Council on Tuesday, warning that wars, arms races, climate shocks and the erosion of international law are placing intense pressure on the multilateral system established to prevent a third world war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Wars and geopolitical divisions constitute ‘dangerous erosion’ of  world order, warns UN chief

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Founding principles of the UN facing ‘profound strain’, Guterres warns
events.description: The Security Council is meeting for a high-level debate on strengthening the UN-centred international system amid mounting pressure on multilateralism and deepening geopolitical divisions. Discussions are expected to focus on defending the UN Charter, reforming global governance and restoring confidence in the Security Council’s ability to respond to international crises.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Founding principles of the UN facing ‘profound strain’, Guterres warns

events.title: One killed and nine missing after chemical explosion at US paper mill
events.description: Nine others were injured and another nine are missing after a tank ruptured at a paper mill in Longview, Washington.

events.title: Suspected Ebola cases in DRC pass 900 as health workers face attacks and shortages
events.description: WHO says outbreak poses ‘very high’ risk for country, but risk of disease spreading globally remains low Authorities in the Democratic Republic of the Congo say that suspected Ebola cases have now passed 900 in the ongoing outbreak in the east of the country. The DRC’s ministry of communication said in a post on X on Sunday that there were 904 suspected cases and 119 suspected deaths. Continue reading...

events.title: Laos: 5 people stuck in cave for a week found alive
events.description: Two of the seven people who became trapped in a flooded cave a week ago are still missing, rescuers have said.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Laos: 5 people stuck in cave for a week found alive

events.title: Venezuela inmates occupy prison roof and set fire to mattresses to highlight alleged abuse
events.description: Inmates at Barinas prison allege they were peacefully protesting when prison staff opened fire, leaving some wounded Inmates at Venezuela’s western Barinas prison staged a protest on its roof on Sunday, piling flaming mattresses and calling for the removal of the facility’s director, whom they accused of overseeing guards as they shot unarmed prisoners. “We want justice. They are shooting us, the guards and the wardens,” a prisoner said in a video shared by the Venezuelan Observatory of Prisons, a local NGO, on X, in which a man is seen with a bullet wound in his chest. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Venezuela inmates occupy prison roof and set fire to mattresses to highlight alleged abuse

events.title: Russia threatens more strikes against Kyiv after drone and missile attack
events.description: As Kyiv recovers from a Russian attack that hit every district in the Ukrainian capital, Ukraine continues long-range strikes on Russia's war machine even as the Kremlin threatens more strikes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia threatens more strikes against Kyiv after drone and missile attack

events.title: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
events.description: Director general of World Health Organization urges neighbouring countries to take immediate action The World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease. “We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment the epidemic is outpacing us,” said the WHO’s director-general, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, as he urged neighbouring countries to take immediate action. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO

events.title: Thai rescuers join effort to free seven people trapped in Laos cave
events.description: Group have been stuck in flooded cave in central Laos for five days after heavy rain caused landslides Divers who helped in the dramatic rescue of a young Thai football team in 2018 have joined efforts to free seven people who have been trapped for five days inside a remote, flooded cave in central Laos. The group entered the cave in Xaysomboun province on Wednesday to hunt for wildlife and search for gold, reports suggest. Heavy rain led to landslides, which blocked the cave entrance. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Thai rescuers join effort to free seven people trapped in Laos cave

events.title: New York and New Jersey subpoena FIFA in World Cup ticket sales investigation
events.description: The US states of New York and New Jersey on Wednesday launched an investigation into FIFA over its ticket sales for the World Cup, with officials alleging the international football association of "fake scarcity and impossibly high prices".

events.title: US ally pledges support for Trump's push to break Iran's grip on Hormuz: 'We are ready to contribute'
events.description: Czech Foreign Minister Macinka voices strong support for Trump, defends increased European defense spending and warns Iran poses a global nuclear threat.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US ally pledges support for Trump's push to break Iran's grip on Hormuz: 'We are ready to contribute'

events.title: At least 82 killed after massive gas explosion rips through coal mine in China
events.description: A massive gas explosion ripped through a coal mine in China's Shanxi province, killing at least 82 people and hospitalizing 120 others, the AP reports.

events.title: Bangladesh seeks IMF aid: How badly has Iran war hit its economy?
events.description: The International Monetary Fund warns that Iran war could trigger a spike in global debt levels.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Bangladesh seeks IMF aid: How badly has Iran war hit its economy?

events.title: Ukraine: UN alarmed by reports of deadly strike on dormitory in occupied Luhansk
events.description: The United Nations voiced alarm on Friday over reports of an overnight attack on a vocational school and dormitory in the town of Starobilsk in Ukraine’s Luhansk region which killed and injured multiple civilians, including children.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: UN alarmed by reports of deadly strike on dormitory in occupied Luhansk

events.title: Ed Husic calls on Labor to establish ‘red line’ on Israel’s actions, including treatment of flotilla activists
events.description: ‘At some point the statements have got to end and the action needs to begin’, member for Chifley says Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor MP Ed Husic says Australia has tolerated poor behaviour from the Israeli government for too long, calling for a “red line” from Canberra including tougher sanctions, an end to defence cooperation and a block on supplying parts for the F-35 fighter jet . Husic - the former industry and science minister – said a pattern of bad behaviour from the government of Israel’s prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, included mistreatment of activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla, the death of Australian aid worker Zomi Frankcom and the destruction of Australian war graves . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ed Husic calls on Labor to establish ‘red line’ on Israel’s actions, including treatment of flotilla activists

events.title: DR Congo facing 'catastrophic collision' of Ebola and war, WHO chief warns
events.description: The ongoing conflict between armed groups in the eastern Democratic Republic of Congo has made it harder for health workers to contain the worsening Ebola outbreak, World Health Organization Secretary-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said Wednesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 DR Congo facing 'catastrophic collision' of Ebola and war, WHO chief warns

events.title: Israel kills new Hamas military leader in Gaza
events.description: Israel's defence ministry said Wednesday they had targeted and killed the new leader of Hamas's military wing, Mohammed Odeh, during air strikes in Gaza City, less than two weeks after killing his predecessor. Defence Minister Israel Katz called him “one of the architects” of the October 7 attacks that triggered more than two years of war in Gaza.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel kills new Hamas military leader in Gaza

events.title: India news: Modi warns of heat wave dangers
events.description: Heatstroke has killed at least 16 people in southern Telangana state, and Prime Minister Modi has said Indians must heed warning signs. The heat wave has also sparked forest fires in the north.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India news: Modi warns of heat wave dangers

events.title: Cuba calls for global solidarity amid fears of US aggression
events.description: Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez warned the UN Security Council that US sanctions were pushing the island toward a "humanitarian catastrophe" as tensions with Washington escalate.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Cuba calls for global solidarity amid fears of US aggression

events.title: Brazilian candidate Flavio Bolsonaro asks Trump to designate crime groups as terrorists
events.description: Brazilian conservative presidential hopeful Flavio Bolsonaro said on Tuesday he asked US President Donald Trump during a White House meeting to designate Brazil's two largest criminal groups, Comando Vermelho (CV) and Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC), as terrorist organisations, arguing they meet the criteria for the label.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Brazilian candidate Flavio Bolsonaro asks Trump to designate crime groups as terrorists

events.title: 5 villagers missing in a flooded Laos cave for more than a week have been found alive
events.description: Five villagers trapped in a flooded cave in central Laos for over a week have been found alive
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 5 villagers missing in a flooded Laos cave for more than a week have been found alive

events.title: Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro
events.description: The World Food Programme (WFP) has deplored a Russian attack on a warehouse in Dnipro, Ukraine that destroyed “a significant quantity” of food aid destined for thousands in frontline areas.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro

events.title: Ebola epidemic spreading rapidly and outpacing containment efforts
events.description: There are more than 900 suspected cases of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and 220 suspected deaths, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), Tedros Ghebreyesus, said on Monday.

events.title: Gunmaker lawsuit after Auburn grad’s death faces major obstacle, attorney warns
events.description: Auburn graduate Whitney Robeson's family sues gunmaker over alleged accidental shooting, but attorney says antique revolver claim faces legal hurdles.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gunmaker lawsuit after Auburn grad’s death faces major obstacle, attorney warns

events.title: Canada to order military plane fleet from Sweden in shift from US suppliers
events.description: Mark Carney announces purchase of Saab’s GlobalEye early warning aircraft to patrol Arctic territory Canada has announced plans to buy a fleet of early warning planes from Sweden’s Saab rather than a competing option from Boeing, as the country seeks to reduce reliance on US defense firms. Mark Carney, the prime minister, said on Wednesday that Canada would opt for Saab’s GlobalEye, which is based on Bombardier’s Global 6500 jet. Boeing’s E-7 Wedgetail plane – which has suffered from delays and cost overruns – had also been in contention. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canada to order military plane fleet from Sweden in shift from US suppliers

events.title: Five people stuck in flooded cave in Laos for week found alive
events.description: Five people who were stuck in a flooded cave in central Laos for more than a week have been found alive, but two others are still missing, rescuers say.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Five people stuck in flooded cave in Laos for week found alive

events.title: U.S. military kills alleged narco-terrorist in lethal strike on drug-trafficking vessel in Eastern Pacific
events.description: U.S. military strikes a suspected drug-trafficking vessel in the Eastern Pacific, killing an alleged narco-terrorist, SOUTHCOM says on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. military kills alleged narco-terrorist in lethal strike on drug-trafficking vessel in Eastern Pacific

events.title: US embassy in Ukraine warns of 'potentially significant air attack' that could happen in next 24 hours

events.title: FIFA subpoenaed by New York, New Jersey in World Cup ticket investigation

events.title: Iran: Global internet access to reopen after 3-month blackout
events.description: 🇮🇷 Just an empty gesture? Paymon Azmoudeh weighs in on #Iran’s orders to reopen international internet access after a 3-month blackout. More on #F24Debate ➡️https://go.france24.com/XiV5

events.title: Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’
events.description: The overnight Russian attack on Ukraine, which centred on the capital, Kyiv, reportedly involved some 90 missiles, including a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile and 60 drones. The senior UN official in the country called for an end to civilian harm.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’

events.title: World Urban Forum backs ‘Baku Call to Action’ on global housing crisis
events.description: A landmark gathering of more than 57,000 participants – the largest in the history of the World Urban Forum – closed on Friday in Baku with an urgent call to rethink how the world houses its people, as a new roadmap urges governments, cities and communities to act collectively on a crisis affecting billions.

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Human cost of Ukraine war, a pattern that ‘defies’ international law, says senior UN aid official
events.description: The Security Council is meeting at the request of Russia which on Friday accused Ukraine of targeting a student dormitory overnight in the occupied Luhansk region, reportedly killing six people – including children – and injuring dozens. Kyiv has denied targeting the civilian building, saying it had struck a Russian military drone command headquarters, according to news reports. Follow live coverage below and for full meetings coverage of the council, click here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Human cost of Ukraine war, a pattern that ‘defies’ international law, says senior UN aid official

events.title: Ebola risk ‘very high’ in eastern DR Congo as UN intensifies response
events.description: The UN is rushing emergency personnel, funding and supplies into eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) to counter the fast-growing Ebola outbreak spreading through conflict-ravaged provinces.

events.title: All 50,000 evacuees cleared to return home after chemical tank crisis at Southern California aerospace plant
events.description: All evacuation orders from a chemical emergency at a GKN Aerospace facility in Garden Grove, California, were lifted Tuesday after the tank stabilized.

events.title: Deadly suicide blast rips through Pakistan train route, killing at least 23
events.description: At least 23 killed and 70 wounded after a suicide bomber reportedly detonated an explosives-laden vehicle alongside an incoming passenger train in Quetta, Pakistan.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Deadly suicide blast rips through Pakistan train route, killing at least 23

events.title: Germany: Alleged leftist militant found guilty of robbery
events.description: Daniela Klette, suspected to be one of the last remaining members of the left-wing terrorist group Red Army Faction, has been found guilty for her part in a series of robberies — but more serious charges await.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany: Alleged leftist militant found guilty of robbery

events.title: Witnesses accuse the Israeli army of arbitrary arrests in Syria
events.description: The Israeli army has arrested at least 197 men in southern Syria since late 2024. A lawyer confirmed that 43 of them are still being held in what he says is arbitrary detention in Israeli prisons. The Israeli army, for its part, says they are targeting “suspected terrorists”.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Witnesses accuse the Israeli army of arbitrary arrests in Syria

events.title: Australian police plan to form a heavily armed team in response to Bondi Beach massacre
events.description: An Australian government inquiry has heard that a state police force has worked to form a heavily armed rapid response team since the Bondi Beach shooting
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Australian police plan to form a heavily armed team in response to Bondi Beach massacre

events.description: The eastern Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) "faces a catastrophic collision of disease and conflict" as the Ebola outbreak continues, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has said.

events.title: Greetings from Cape Verde, where the sounds of samba, jazz and morna fill the air
events.description: Music is interwoven with the sounds of daily life in this West African island nation, which hosted two international music festivals in April and has been named the African Capital of Culture for 2028.

events.description: Temperature records are breaking and triggering government warnings, with London hitting 95 degrees the past two days. Experts say unpredictable and extreme weather is becoming more frequent.

events.title: U.S. strikes Iran. And, immigration courts use new tactic to speed up deportations

events.title: Israeli strikes kill dozens in southern Lebanon as Netanyahu expands ground campaign

events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korea says it tested new warheads, technology and navigation in latest launches

events.title: Muslims worldwide celebrate Eid al-Adha, the Festival of Sacrifice
events.description: The sacred holiday coincides with Hajj, drawing over 1.7 million pilgrims seeking spiritual fulfilment in Saudi Arabia.

events.title: President Sheinbaum allows Iran team to stay in Mexico during World Cup after US refusal
events.description: Fifa approached Mexico after US declined to host Iran squad despite it playing group games in the United States Mexico’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum , said on Monday her government agreed to allow the Iranian national football team to stay in Mexico during the World Cup , adding that the US did not want to host the team. The team will still play its group stage matches in the US but its base has been moved to Tijuana, Mexico, just south of San Diego, California, a move that Iran’s football federation announced recently and that was confirmed by Fifa, the sport’s governing body. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran condemns US strikes as 'gross violation' of ceasefire

events.title: USAID was shut down 'to soothe ego' of Musk, whistleblower says
events.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, Nicolas Enrich, a former senior USAID official-turned-whistleblower, said the US agency was dismantled not to cut waste but "to soothe the ego of the world's richest man", then DOGE head Elon Musk. Enrich warned that up to 14 million people could die in the next five years as a result and called the shutdown "illegal".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 USAID was shut down 'to soothe ego' of Musk, whistleblower says

events.title: ‘Makes no sense’: experts doubt pause in US arms sale to Taiwan is due to Iran war
events.description: While approval is due soon for $14bn deal, actual deliveries to Taiwan are years away – making ‘Operation Epic Fury’ in the Gulf an unlikely cause The Trump administration’s war against Iran should have no impact on arms sales to Taiwan, experts have said, after a US official suggested a pause in the delivery of a key weapons package was due to the Gulf conflict. Analysts told the Guardian that a $14bn arms package left in limbo after Donald Trump’s meeting with Xi Jinping could take up to six years to process, and there was a “low likelihood” of any true connection between events in Iran and weapons delivery to Taiwan. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Makes no sense’: experts doubt pause in US arms sale to Taiwan is due to Iran war

events.title: One killed, nine missing as 3.4m-litre chemical tank implodes in US
events.description: At least one person has been killed and nine others are unaccounted for after a chemical tank imploded in the US.

events.title: Gaza: Life-saving medicines blocked as killing continues, disease gains ground

events.title: Washington paper mill chemical tank implodes, killing at least 1 and leaving 9 missing
events.description: At least one person was killed and nine remain unaccounted for after a chemical tank imploded at a paper mill in Longview, Washington, authorities said.

events.title: Crash involving speeding train, minibus in Belgium leaves 4 dead including 2 children
events.description: Four people, including two children, were killed when a passenger train hit a minibus at a crossing near Buggenhout, Belgium, during the morning rush.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: US judge dismisses criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García
events.description: Judge said Trump administration would not have prosecuted Ábrego had he not challenged his high-profile deportation Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A US judge dismissed a criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García on Friday, finding that the Trump administration would not have prosecuted him had he not challenged his high-profile deportation. Ábrego, who had entered the United States without authorization in the past, became a symbol of the Trump administration’s drive for mass deportations when he was sent to the notorious anti-terrorism mega-prison in El Salvador known as Cecot in March 2025. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 US judge dismisses criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García

events.title: ICC trial for ex-Philippine President Duterte to start in November
events.description: Judges at the International Criminal Court have set Nov. 30 as the start date for the trial of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte for crimes against humanity

events.title: Middle East update: Airstrikes and evacuation orders in Lebanon, aid restrictions in Gaza

events.title: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing

events.title: Samsung memory chip staff in line for £310,000 bonuses after AI profit-sharing deal
events.description: Agreement averts strike and shows latest impact of AI boom as two more chipmakers join $1tn club Employees at Samsung Electronics’ memory chip division are to receive bonuses averaging about £310,000 each through a landmark profit-sharing agreement, as the AI boom drives up chipmakers’ profits. Fears of a strike at Samsung were averted on Wednesday after two unions for the world’s largest memory chipmaker said 74% of the 62,616 workers who cast their votes had backed the deal. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘She does not back down’: the couple seeking to legalise same-sex marriage in Botswana
events.description: Bonolo Selelo and Tsholofelo Kumile are going to court for right to wed but face fierce opposition from church groups Bonolo Selelo was at Botswana’s national museum for a Gaborone Pride event when she spotted Tsholofelo Kumile and was struck by her good looks. The two initiated a conversation and when Kumile expressed anxiety about what a tarot reading at the event might hold, Selelo thought nothing of offering her a hug. The reading turned out positive but Kumile claimed her hug anyway and they talked for hours. That was 1 October 2023. Two months later, they moved in together. Then, on a hike during the Easter holidays in 2024, Selelo proposed to Kumile. A year later, they visited a local government office to register their intent to marry and were told it wasn’t legal. Continue reading...

events.title: Turkey: Leadership dispute engulfs opposition CHP
events.description: The top figures in Turkey's main opposition party differ on how to resolve the leadership vacuum after a court annulled its 2023 primary. One wants the party delegates to pick a leader, the other wants members to vote.

events.title: For Pakistan, joining Abraham Accords is a tricky choice
events.description: US President Donald Trump wants Pakistan and some other Muslim-majority nations to normalize ties with Israel. While joining the Abraham Accords has its benefits for Islamabad, the repercussions would be immense.

events.title: Spain: Police raid HQ of ruling PSOE Socialist party in graft probe
events.description: Police have searched the headquarters of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's PSOE party investigating possible illegal payments. Sanchez meanwhile said he was still standing by former PM Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero.

events.title: Chinese dissident found at sea detained in South Korea
events.description: Dong Guangping, a former policeman who has been jailed for his opposition to China's Communist regime, was found drifting in a small rubber boat off South Korea's west coast.

events.title: License to play: The new James Bond video game
events.description: James Bond is back, and he's as charismatic and daring as ever. The video game "007 First Light" tells the origin story of the British secret agent — and the stakes are high.

events.title: The German gym fighting the far right with MMA
events.description: The far right is increasingly using MMA to recruit new members. But a gym in Chemnitz, in the east of Germany, is fighting back with a martial arts program focused on diversity and empowerment.

events.title: Cockroach Janta Party: India's youth turn judge's jab into viral political movement
events.description: A satirical political movement has gone viral amongst India's youth with an unusual mascot at the helm: the cockroach. The Cockroach Janta Party was born after India's chief justice referred to unemployed youth as "cockroaches". Though he later said he was misquoted, his comments ricocheted online. As outrage escalated, 30-year-old Abhijeet Dipke asked: "What if all cockroaches come together?" The online satirical movement now has nearly 23 million Instagram followers and counting.

events.title: Film show: French WWII epic 'De Gaulle: Resistance' hits screens
events.description: Ben Croll takes us through four films from the Cannes Film Festival which are being released in cinemas this week. One of France's biggest film events of the year is Antonin Baudry's "De Gaulle: Resistance", the first of a two-part WWII epic centred on French general Charles de Gaulle, from the fall of France in 1940 to the liberation in 1945. Ben tells us why this film is part of a new trend of French blockbusters focused on uniquely French stories.

events.title: FRANCE 24's live streams are temporarily unavailable on its digital platforms
events.description: A technical incident is preventing FRANCE 24 from offering its live broadcast on digital platforms.

events.title: Critically endangered New Zealand parakeet gets lifeline from randy pair
events.description: A critically endangered New Zealand parakeet known as kakariki karaka has seen its numbers surge thanks to a pair of super breeders now responsible for more than 10 percent of the total population. Parents Nacho and Trixie were paired up in 2024 at the Isaac Conservation and Wildlife Trust in Christchurch and have since produced 55 chicks, including 33 this year alone.

events.title: French luxury store Galeries Lafayette shuts Beijing flagship branch after 13 years
events.description: French luxury retailer Galeries Lafayette closed its flagship Beijing store on Wednesday after 13 years, underscoring weak consumer spending and changing shopping habits in China. Shoppers snapped up final bargains as staff cleared unsold goods and dismantled displays at the six-floor department store before its indefinite closure.

events.title: Germany's most-wanted woman, 67, jailed after decades on run
events.description: A former member of the notorious Baader-Meinhof gang has been jailed for a string of armed robberies after decades on the run.

events.title: Spanish police search prime minister's party HQ over corruption allegations
events.description: Police have searched the Madrid headquarters of Spain's ruling Socialist Party over a potential illegal payments scheme.

events.title: How unwell is Donald Trump?
events.description: 👉 Follow Trump100 on your podcast app 👈

events.title: Why Russia is 'relentlessly targeting' the UK
events.description: 👉 Listen to This Is Why on your podcast app 👈

events.title: Meeting the extraordinary Gisele Pelicot
events.description: To meet Gisele Pelicot is to meet an icon.

events.title: Ghana begins repatriating citizens from South Africa due to anti-immigration tensions
events.description: Ghana has started a voluntary repatriation program for its citizens as a reaction to anti-immigration tensions in South Africa

events.title: Dozens of Assad-era chemical weapons found in Syria in recent weeks, watchdog says
events.description: The Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons says it has found dozens of undeclared chemical bombs and rockets in Syria
events.ticker_label: 💥 Dozens of Assad-era chemical weapons found in Syria in recent weeks, watchdog says

events.title: Muslim pilgrims perform Hajj rituals in strong heat as Eid al-Adha celebrations start
events.description: Pilgrims in Saudi Arabia threw pebbles at a large pillar in a symbolic ritual during the final days of Hajj

events.title: Climbers meet in Nepal to discuss the challenges of scaling Mount Everest
events.description: Climbers and officials have met in Kathmandu to discuss the risks of scaling Mount Everest

events.title: Philippine bishop and ex-ICC judge lead new inquiry into thousands of Duterte-era killings
events.description: A group led by a Roman Catholic bishop in the Philippines has launched a fact-finding body to document accounts of ex-President Rodrigo Duterte’s anti-drugs crackdown

events.title: Penpa Tsering sworn in for a second term to lead Tibet’s government-in-exile
events.description: Penpa Tsering has been sworn in for a second term as the president of Tibet’s government-in-exile based in India

events.title: Brazil's Flávio Bolsonaro leans into Trump connection as scandal shakes his presidential bid
events.description: Brazilian Sen. Flávio Bolsonaro is in Washington, leaning on his family's connection with former U.S. President Donald Trump

events.title: Longtime GOP senator loses primary after rival's Trump endorsement and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Teen stepbrother accused of killing Anna Kepner on Carnival cruise remains free before trial
events.description: A judge ruled Wednesday that the teenage stepbrother accused of killing 18-year-old Anna Kepner aboard a Carnival cruise ship will remain free while awaiting trial.

events.title: DNA breakthrough leads to arrest in grisly 33-year-old cold case investigators never gave up on
events.description: A 1993 cold case murder in southern Illinois is allegedly solved after forensic genealogy DNA led to first-degree murder charges against a 70-year-old man.

events.title: Murder-suicide probe underway after alleged Tom Selleck impersonator scam targeted woman
events.description: Elderly couple linked to a Tom Selleck romance scam found dead in California home as sheriff's office investigates case as a murder-suicide.

events.title: Israeli ambassador compares France's far-left leader's rhetoric to Hitler as antisemitism surges
events.description: France recorded 1,320 antisemitic acts in 2025, up from 436 in 2022, as Jewish families alter daily habits to avoid being identified, CRIF president says.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israeli ambassador compares France's far-left leader's rhetoric to Hitler as antisemitism surges

events.title: Mass tanker blackout rattles Gulf ahead of 1.35M-barrel oil transfer amid US-Iran talks: firm
events.description: Maritime tracking signals collapse near the UAE's Fujairah oil hub hours before President Trump announced progress on a bilateral peace deal with Iran, according to an AI maritime firm.
